subject: Databases
title: Join Strategies
description: A fast breakdown of Join Strategies for builders.

hook.text: JOIN users ON orders.user_id = users.id. The planner picks from three strategies: nested loop, hash, or merge.

buildup.text: Nested Loop: for each outer row, scan the inner table. Good when the inner table is small or indexed.

discovery.text: Hash Join: build a hash table from one side, probe it with the other. Great for large unsorted tables.

twist.text: Merge Join: both sides must be sorted. It walks through both in order. Very fast when both are pre-sorted.

climax.text: The planner picks the best strategy based on data size and available indexes. You rarely choose manually.

punchline.text: Three join strategies. The planner picks. EXPLAIN tells you which.

quiz.question: When does the planner typically choose a Hash Join?
options: When joining large unsorted tables where building a hash table is cheaper than sorting
When both tables are already sorted
When the join key is a primary key
